instruction: How do I perform the Partner plank band row?
response: The Partner plank band row is an exercise targeting the Abdominals. Equipment needed: Bands. Instructions: The partner plank band row is an abdominal exercise where two partners perform single-arm planks while pulling on the opposite ends of an exercise band. This technique can be done for time or reps in any ab-focused workout.

instruction: How do I perform the Banded crunch isometric hold?
response: The Banded crunch isometric hold is an exercise targeting the Abdominals. Equipment needed: Bands. Instructions: The banded crunch isometric hold is an exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, particularly the rectus abdominis or "six-pack" muscles. The band adds resistance and continuous tension to this popular exercise.

instruction: How do I perform the FYR Banded Plank Jack?
response: The FYR Banded Plank Jack is an exercise targeting the Abdominals. Equipment needed: Bands. Instructions: The banded plank jack is a variation on the plank that involves moving the legs in and out for repetitions. Having a band around the thighs forces the lower body to work harder, particularly the hips and glutes. The plank jack is commonly performed as part of a bodyweight circuit, or as part of a dynamic warm-up.

instruction: How do I perform the Banded crunch?
response: The Banded crunch is an exercise targeting the Abdominals. Equipment needed: Bands. Instructions: The banded crunch is an exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, particularly the rectus abdominis or "six-pack" muscles. instruction: How do I perform the Crunch?
response: The Crunch is an exercise targeting the Abdominals. Equipment needed: Bands. Instructions: The crunch is a popular core exercise targeting the rectus abdominis, or "six-pack" muscles, as well as the obliques. It has been the centerpiece of many ab-focused workouts, due to both its simplicity and the intense burn and mind-muscle connection it produces. It can be performed for time or reps as part of the ab-focused portion of any workout.

instruction: How do I perform the Decline band press sit-up?
response: The Decline band press sit-up is an exercise targeting the Abdominals. Equipment needed: Bands. Instructions: The decline band press sit-up is a weighted core exercise that works the rectus abdominis or "six pack" muscles, as well as the deep core muscles. It also taxes the muscles of the shoulders and upper back. Sit-up variations with added resistance are usually performed for moderate to high reps, such as 8-12 reps per set or more, as part of the core-focused portion of a workout.

instruction: How do I perform the Band low-to-high twist?
response: The Band low-to-high twist is an exercise targeting the Abdominals. Equipment needed: Bands. Instructions: The band low-to-high twist is a core exercise targeting the upper abdominals and the obliques.
